items.title: Psilocybin-assisted therapy in treatment-resistant depression: rapid remission, uncertain durability, and the next phase of clinical evidence.

summaries.en: Treatment-resistant depression (TRD) requires novel interventions beyond conventional antidepressants. A recent review examines psilocybin-assisted therapy, which combines 5-HT2A receptor agonism with structured psychological support in supervised sessions. Early randomized trials show rapid symptom reduction and promising short-term remission rates, though evidence in TRD populations remains limited. Key questions persist: durability beyond weeks, safety of repeated dosing, and comparative efficacy versus established TRD treatments (esketamine, ECT, TMS). Modest sample sizes and short follow-up periods constrain current evidence.
summaries.da: Behandlingsresistent depression (TRD) kræver nye interventioner ud over konventionelle antidepressiva. En recent review undersøger psykedelika-assisteret terapi, som kombinerer 5-HT2A-receptoragonisme med struktureret psykologisk support i overvågede sessioner. Tidlige randomiserede forsøg viser hurtig symptomreduktion og lovende kortsigtede remissionsrater, skønt evidensen i TRD-populationer forbliver begrænset. Vigtige spørgsmål består: varighed efter uger, sikkerhed ved gentagen dosering og sammenlignet effektivitet mod etablerede TRD-behandlinger (esketamin, ECT, TMS). Beskedne stikprøvestørrelser og kort opfølgning begrænser nuværende evidens.

items.title: Epigenetic landscapes of classical psychedelics and ketamine: molecular mechanisms of long-lasting neuromodulation.

summaries.en: A review examining how classical psychedelics (LSD, psilocybin, DMT, mescaline) and ketamine produce lasting neuroplastic changes through epigenetic mechanisms. The study synthesizes evidence on how these compounds alter DNA methylation, histone modifications, chromatin structure, and non-coding RNA dynamics. The authors propose that psychedelics modulate metabolic pathways affecting epigenetic cofactors, potentially driving persistent transcriptional changes and enhanced synaptic connectivity underlying their therapeutic effects, though direct causal evidence remains limited.
summaries.da: En oversigt over, hvordan klassiske psykedelika (LSD, psilocybin, DMT, meskalin) og ketamin producerer varige neuroplastiske ændringer gennem epigenetiske mekanismer. Studiet syntetiserer evidens for, hvordan disse stoffer påvirker DNA-methylering, histonmodifikationer, kromatingstruktur og non-coding RNA-dynamik. Forfatterne foreslår, at psykedelika modulerer metaboliske veje, som påvirker epigenetiske kofaktorer, potentielt drivende vedvarende transkriptionelle ændringer og øget synaptisk forbindelse bag deres terapeutiske virkninger, selvom direkte kausal evidens mangler.

items.title: Psychedelics and the thalamocortical system: A scoping review.

summaries.en: This scoping review (PRISMA method) synthesizes preclinical and clinical evidence on psychedelics' effects on the thalamocortical system. Psychedelics show therapeutic promise for depression, trauma, OCD, and other conditions. Since psychiatric disorders like schizophrenia and autism involve thalamocortical alterations, understanding how psychedelics (NMDA antagonists, 5-HT2A agonists, iboga alkaloids) modulate this system is crucial for both neurobiology and clinical applications.
summaries.da: Dette scoping review (PRISMA-metoden) opsummerer præklinisk og klinisk evidens for psykedelikas effekter på det thalamocorticale system. Psykedelika viser terapeutisk potentiale for depression, trauma, OCD og andre tilstande. Da psykiatriske lidelser som skizofreni og autisme involverer thalamocorticale ændringer, er forståelse af hvordan psykedelika (NMDA-antagonister, 5-HT2A-agonister, iboga-alkaloider) modulerer dette system afgørende for neurobiologi og klinisk praksis.

items.title: Personality changes following first-time psychedelic use in college students in Germany.

summaries.en: A one-year longitudinal study of 102 first-time psychedelic users and 1066 never-users among Berlin university students examined personality changes using the Big Five Inventory. First-time users showed small relative increases in Openness and decreases in Conscientiousness, though effects were attenuated after adjusting for confounders (Openness p=0.06, Conscientiousness p=0.05, both FDR-adjusted p=0.16). Changes were not clearly distinct from first-time use of other illicit substances. Users with psychiatric diagnoses showed larger Neuroticism reductions. Results suggest modest personality shifts following first-time psychedelic use with limited causal interpretability.
summaries.da: Et etårigt longitudinelt studie af 102 førstegangsbrugere af psykedelika og 1066 aldrig-brugere blandt Berlins universitetsstuderende undersøgte personlighedsændringer via Big Five Inventory. Førstegangsbrugere viste små relative stigninger i Åbenhed og fald i Samvittighedsfuldhed, selvom effekterne blev mindre efter justering for forstyrrelsesfaktorer (Åbenhed p=0,06, Samvittighedsfuldhed p=0,05, begge FDR-justeret p=0,16). Ændringer var ikke klart forskellige fra førstegangsbrugelse af andre illegale stoffer. Brugere med psykiatriske diagnoser viste større Neuroticism-reduktioner. Resultater tyder på beskedne personlighedsændringer efter førstegangs psykedelika-brug med begrænset kausal tolkbarhed.

items.title: Navigating 'k-land': a qualitative exploration of participants' experiences of ketamine-assisted psychotherapy for methamphetamine use disorder.

summaries.en: This qualitative study embedded in an open-label pilot trial examined 14 participants' experiences of ketamine-assisted psychotherapy (KAP) for methamphetamine use disorder through semi-structured interviews. Thematic analysis identified four key themes: pragmatic openness to treatment after prior limited success, importance of relational safety and clinical support, reduced emotional reactivity following ketamine sessions, and translation of psychological shifts into behavioural change. Findings illuminate perceived mechanisms and acceptability of KAP for this undertreated condition.
summaries.da: Dette kvalitative studie, indlejret i et åbent pilotstudie, undersøgte 14 deltageres erfaringer med ketamin-assisteret psykoterapi (KAP) for metamfetaminbrug gennem semi-strukturerede interviews. Tematisk analyse identificerede fire centrale temaer: pragmatisk åbenhed over for behandling efter tidligere begrænset succes, betydningen af relationel sikkerhed og klinisk support, reduceret følelsesmæssig reaktivitet efter ketaminsessioner og omsætning af psykologiske skift til adfærdsændringer. Fundet belyser oplevede virkningsmekanismer og acceptabilitet af KAP for denne undertreatet tilstand.

items.title: Psychedelics in Mental Health: Opportunities and Challenges.

summaries.en: A Brain and Behavior special issue examines psychedelics and substance-assisted therapies for treatment-resistant depression, PTSD, and substance use disorders. While early efficacy trials show promise, key questions persist regarding mechanisms, psychological support, safety, and ethics. The collection emphasizes that psychedelic therapy extends beyond pharmacology: therapeutic relationships, preparation, integration, and cultural context significantly shape outcomes. Contributors stress that subjective experience and meaning-making are central to therapeutic change, not incidental features.
summaries.da: Et særnummer af Brain and Behavior undersøger psykedelika og stoffassisteret terapi for behandlingsresistent depression, PTSD og stoffmisbrug. Selvom tidlige effektivitetsstudier viser lovende resultater, gennemgår området store udfordringer omkring mekanismer, psykologisk support, sikkerhed og etik. Samlingen understreger, at psykedelika-terapi går ud over farmakologi: terapeutiske relationer, forberedelse, integration og kulturel kontekst former væsentligt resultaterne. Bidragyderne fremhæver, at subjektiv erfaring og meningsudvikling er centrale for terapeutisk forandring.

items.title: Convergent increases in serotonin 1B receptor binding following ketamine and electroconvulsive therapy: a multi-centre bayesian re-analysis of PET data.

summaries.en: Multi-centre PET study using Bayesian re-analysis examining serotonin 1B receptor binding following ketamine and electroconvulsive therapy. Both treatments showed convergent increases in receptor binding, suggesting shared neurobiological mechanisms despite different administration routes. Findings support the role of serotonergic systems in treatment response for depression.
summaries.da: Multi-center PET-studie med Bayesiansk re-analyse af serotoninbinding til 1B-receptorer efter ketamin og elektrokonvulsiv terapi. Begge behandlinger viste sammenfaldende stigning i receptorbinding, hvilket tyder på delte neurobiologiske mekanismer på trods af forskellige administrationsveje. Resultaterne understøtter serotonerge systemers rolle i behandlingsrespons ved depression.

items.title: Convergent increases in serotonin 1B receptor binding following ketamine and electroconvulsive therapy: a multi-centre bayesian re-analysis of PET data

summaries.en: Multi-centre PET study (n=222) using [11C]AZ10419369 examined serotonin 1B receptor binding in depression patients before and after ketamine (n=19), placebo (n=10), or ECT (n=13). Hierarchical Bayesian analysis revealed significant increases in binding following ketamine (6.4%, 95% CI: 3.1-9.6%) and ECT (9.3%, 95% CI: 4.3-14.2%), both distinguishable from placebo. Changes were independent of symptom improvement, suggesting serotonergic mechanisms may contribute to these treatments' antidepressive effects.
summaries.da: Multicenterstudie (n=222) med PET-scanning [11C]AZ10419369 undersøgte serotoninbindinger hos depressionspatienters før og efter ketamin (n=19), placebo (n=10) eller ECT (n=13). Hierarkisk Bayesiansk analyse viste signifikante stigninger i bindinger efter ketamin (6,4%, 95% CI: 3,1-9,6%) og ECT (9,3%, 95% CI: 4,3-14,2%), begge skelnes fra placebo. Ændringer var uafhængige af symptomforbedring, hvilket antyder, at serotonerge mekanismer kan bidrage til disse behandlingers antidepressive effekter.

items.title: CSF galanin and noradrenaline downregulation by psilocybin therapy in major depressive disorder.

summaries.en: A secondary analysis of a randomized, placebo-controlled trial examined cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers in major depressive disorder patients treated with psilocybin. The study found that psilocybin selectively reduced CSF galanin and noradrenaline levels, suggesting that normalization of these co-transmitters may represent a key mechanism underlying psilocybin's rapid antidepressant effects.
summaries.da: En sekundæranalyse af et randomiseret, placebokontrolleret forsøg undersøgte cerebrospinalvæske-biomarkører hos patienter med depression behandlet med psykedelika. Studiet viste, at psykedelika selektivt reducerede galanin og noradrenalin i cerebrospinalvæsken, hvilket tyder på, at normalisering af disse co-transmittere kan være en nøglemekanisme bag psykedelikas hurtige antidepressive effekt.

items.title: α

summaries.en: This study examines how classic serotonergic psychedelics like psilocybin function as agonists at cortical serotonin 5-HT2A receptors, a key mechanism underlying their psychoactive effects. The research clarifies the receptor-level basis of psychedelics' neurobiological action.
summaries.da: Studiet undersøger, hvordan klassiske serotoninerge psykedelika som psilocybin virker som agonister på kortikale serotoninreceptorer (5-HT2A), en vigtig mekanisme bag deres psykoaktive effekter. Forskningen præciserer det neurobiologiske grundlag for psykedelikas virkemåde på receptorniveau.

items.title: Psilocybin therapy for adult females with anorexia nervosa: pilot study.

summaries.en: Pilot study (n=21 females) examined psilocybin therapy for anorexia nervosa using a single-blind, within-individual design. Participants received three oral psilocybin doses (1mg, 25mg, 25mg) over 6 weeks alongside talk therapy. Primary outcomes measured eating disorder symptoms (EDE) and treatment motivation (RMQ) at 6 weeks, 3 and 6 months. Psilocybin was well tolerated; common adverse events were headache, nausea, and dizziness. Results suggest potential feasibility and safety for psychedelics in anorexia nervosa treatment.
summaries.da: Pilotundersøgelse (n=21 kvinder) undersøgte psykedelika-terapi for anoreksia nervosa med single-blind, intra-individuelt design. Deltagere modtog tre orale psykedelika-doser (1mg, 25mg, 25mg) over 6 uger sammen med samtalebehandling. Primære målinger var spiseforstyrrelsessymptomer (EDE) og behandlingsmotivation (RMQ) ved 6 uger, 3 og 6 måneder. Psykedelika blev godt tolereret; hyppigste bivirkninger var hovedpine, kvalme og svimmelhed. Resultater tyder på potentiel sikkerhed og gennemførbarhed af psykedelika til anoreksia nervosa-behandling.

items.title: Association between Intravenous Ketamine Treatment and Cognitive Function in Patients with Treatment-Resistant Depression: A Double-Blind, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial.

summaries.en: A double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial examined cognitive effects of intravenous ketamine in 34 Japanese patients with treatment-resistant depression. Participants received four doses of ketamine (0.5 mg/kg) or placebo. No significant differences emerged between groups on objective or subjective cognitive function measures. However, ketamine responders (≥50% MADRS reduction) showed improved subjective cognition versus non-responders. Baseline inhibitory control deficits predicted stronger depressive symptom improvement.
summaries.da: Et dobbeltblindet, randomiseret, placebokontrolleret studie undersøgte kognitive effekter af intravenøst ketamin hos 34 japanske patienter med behandlingsresistent depression. Deltagerne modtog fire doser ketamin (0,5 mg/kg) eller placebo. Ingen signifikante forskelle fandtes mellem grupperne på objektive eller subjektive kognitiv funktionsmål. Ketamin-respondenter (≥50% MADRS-reduktion) viste dog forbedret subjektiv kognition sammenlignet med ikke-respondenter. Nedsat inhibitorisk kontrol ved baseline forudsagde større forbedring af depressive symptomer.

items.title: Beyond Enthusiasm: Ayahuasca and the Future of Depression Research.

summaries.en: This article examines ayahuasca's potential in depression treatment beyond preliminary enthusiasm. While psychedelics show promise in clinical research, the piece likely emphasizes the need for rigorous study designs, standardized protocols, and long-term outcome data to establish efficacy and safety profiles in depression populations.
summaries.da: Artiklen undersøger ayahuascas potentiale i depressionsbehandling uden for preliminær entusiasme. Selvom psykedelika viser lovende resultater i klinisk forskning, understreger artiklen sandsynligvis behovet for stringente studieopbygninger, standardiserede protokoller og langsigtede resultater for at fastslå effektivitet og sikkerhedsprofil.

items.title: MDMA-assisted PTSD and Alcohol Therapy Trial (MPATHY): study protocol for a double-blind, randomised, controlled outpatient trial of MDMA-assisted integrated exposure-based therapy for comorbid post-traumatic stress disorder and alcohol use disorder.

summaries.en: The MPATHY trial is a double-blind, randomized controlled study examining MDMA-assisted integrated exposure therapy for comorbid PTSD and alcohol use disorder. The study will randomize 100 participants to receive either COPE (12 sessions) combined with MDMA (80-160 mg) or COPE combined with niacin control. Primary outcomes include PTSD severity and heavy drinking days per week. This world-first trial aims to improve treatment outcomes for this challenging comorbidity, where standard interventions show clinically significant improvement in only ~50% of participants.
summaries.da: MPATHY-studiet er et dobbeltblindet, randomiseret kontrolleret forsøg, der undersøger psykedelika-assisteret integrated eksposuringsterapi for komorbid PTSD og alkoholmisbrug. Studiet randomiserer 100 deltagere til enten COPE (12 sessioner) kombineret med MDMA (80-160 mg) eller COPE kombineret med niacin-kontrol. Primære målmål inkluderer PTSD-sværhedsgrad og tunge drikkedage per uge. Dette verdensførste forsøg sigter mod at forbedre behandlingsresultater for denne udfordrende komorbiditet, hvor standardbehandlinger viser klinisk betydelig forbedring hos kun ~50% af deltagerne.

items.title: Policy pathways and historical insights: Canada's evolving approach to psychedelic access for end-of-life distress.

summaries.en: This policy analysis examines Canada's shifting regulatory stance toward psychedelics in end-of-life care, tracing movement away from prohibition toward clinical recognition. The review contextualizes Canadian developments within international trends in the US, Australia, and Europe, highlighting Canada's unique positioning through its palliative care leadership, medical assistance in dying framework, and federal flexibility mechanisms. Provincial variations and public advocacy are reshaping policy, though heterogeneous drug regulations across jurisdictions reflect ongoing political contingency in reform efforts.
summaries.da: Denne politikanalyse undersøger Canadas skiftende reguleringstilgang til psykedelika i end-of-life behandling, idet den dokumenterer bevægelse væk fra forbud mod klinisk anerkendelse. Analysen kontekstualiserer canadiske udviklingen inden for internationale trends i USA, Australien og Europa og fremhæver Canadas unikke position gennem dets palliativ omsorgslederskab, medicinkvalificeret dødshjælp og føderale fleksibilismekanismer. Provinsielle variationer og patientadvokati omformer politik, selvom heterogene stoffregulativer afspejler vedvarende politisk betingethed i reformbestræbelser.

items.title: Compass Closes Out Phase 3 With 6-Month COMP006 Data, Eyes H1’27 Launch

summaries.en: Compass Pathways released 26-week data from COMP006 Part B, a Phase 3 trial (n=581) evaluating COMP360 psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression. Participants received two doses of 1 mg, 10 mg, or 25 mg three weeks apart. Results support a potential H1'27 regulatory submission for psychedelics-assisted therapy.
summaries.da: Compass Pathways offentliggjorde 26-ugers data fra COMP006 Part B, et fase 3-forsøg (n=581) evaluerende COMP360 psilocybin til behandlingsresistent depression. Deltagere modtog to doser à 1 mg, 10 mg eller 25 mg med tre ugers mellemrum. Resultaterne understøtter potentiel regulatorisk ansøgning i H1'27 for psykedelika-assisteret terapi.

items.title: Ketamine alters the aperiodic EEG exponent in major depression: implications for cortical E/I balance and treatment prediction.

summaries.en: A placebo-controlled study of 24 MDD patients examined whether subanesthetic ketamine modulates the EEG aperiodic exponent, a potential biomarker of cortical excitation-inhibition balance. Patients received IV ketamine (0.54 mg/kg) with resting-state EEG recorded pre-, during, and 24h post-infusion. Ketamine significantly reduced the aperiodic exponent across scalp regions. Higher pretreatment occipital exponent values predicted treatment response (≥33% symptom reduction). These findings suggest the aperiodic exponent may serve as a non-invasive biomarker for predicting ketamine response in treatment-resistant depression.
summaries.da: Et placebo-kontrolleret studie med 24 MDD-patienter undersøgte, om subanæstetisk ketamin modulerer EEG's aperiodiske eksponent, en potentiel biomarkør for kortikalbalance mellem eksitation og inhibition. Patienterne fik IV ketamin (0,54 mg/kg) med EEG-måling før, under og 24 timer efter infusion. Ketamin reducerede signifikant den aperiodiske eksponent på tværs af skalpen. Højere præbehandlings-eksponentværdier i okciputalregionen forudsagde behandlingssvar (≥33% symptomreduktion). Disse fund tyder på, at den aperiodiske eksponent kan fungere som en ikke-invasiv biomarkør til forudsigelse af ketamins effekt ved behandlingsresistent depression.

items.title: Effectiveness and safety of repeat dose subcutaneous ketamine for treatment-resistant depression, and the impact of prior ketamine treatment: open label extension of the KADS study.

summaries.en: Open-label extension of the KADS trial evaluated repeated subcutaneous ketamine for treatment-resistant depression. 130 participants received a 4-week course of racemic ketamine (0.5–0.9 mg/kg twice-weekly). Response rates were modest: 30% at treatment end and 17% at 4-week follow-up. Prior ketamine exposure showed transient reduced initial response but similar long-term outcomes. Over 50% achieved less than 25% symptom reduction, suggesting limited effectiveness in this population.
summaries.da: Open-label extension af KADS-studiet evaluerede gentagen subkutan ketamin til behandlingsresistent depression. 130 deltagere modtog et 4-ugers forløb med racemisk ketamin (0,5–0,9 mg/kg to gange ugentligt). Responsrater var modest: 30% ved behandlingens afslutning og 17% ved 4-ugers opfølgning. Tidligere ketamin-eksponering viste forbigående reduceret initialrespons men lignende langsigtede resultater. Over 50% opnåede mindre end 25% symptomreduktion, hvilket tyder på begrænset effektivitet i denne population.

items.title: Kynurenine pathway profiles as markers of ketamine response in treatment-resistant depression.

summaries.en: This study examined kynurenine pathway metabolites as potential biomarkers for ketamine response in treatment-resistant depression. Thirty patients received six intravenous ketamine infusions over three weeks, with serum metabolites measured at baseline, day 3, and day 18. Higher baseline kynurenic acid levels were associated with greater symptom improvement on the Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale, suggesting the kynurenine pathway may help identify patients most likely to benefit from ketamine treatment.
summaries.da: Studiet undersøgte kynurenin-stofvekselsproduker som potentielle biomarkører for ketamins virkning ved behandlingsresistent depression. Tredive patienter modtog seks intravenøse ketamin-infusioner over tre uger, med målinger af serum-stofveksler på dag 1, dag 3 og dag 18. Højere basale niveauer af kynurensyre var forbundet med større symptomforbedring på Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale, hvilket indikerer, at kynurenin-stofvekselen kan hjælpe med at identificere patienter, der mest sandsynligt vil profitere af ketamin-behandling.

items.title: High-order brain interactions during ketamine-induced state changes: A functional marker of response in late-life treatment-resistant depression?

summaries.en: This secondary analysis of a randomized, double-blind, midazolam-controlled trial examined high-order brain interactions (HOI) in 30 late-life veterans with treatment-resistant depression following ketamine infusion (0.1-0.5 mg/kg, n=18) or midazolam control (n=12). EEG-derived O-information—a metric capturing synergistic and redundant interactions across brain regions—showed temporally dynamic alterations, with maximal effects in the alpha-band at 1 hour (Cohen's d=2.57). Greater increases in 24-hour alpha redundancy correlated with symptom improvement at day 7 (β=69.31, q=0.05), suggesting HOI may serve as a neural marker of ketamine response in late-life treatment-resistant depression.
summaries.da: Denne sekundæranalyse af et randomiseret, dobbeltblindet, midazolam-kontrolleret forsøg undersøgte højordens hjernefunktioner hos 30 ældre veteraner med behandlingsresistent depression efter ketamininfusion (0,1-0,5 mg/kg, n=18) eller midazolam-kontrol (n=12). EEG-afledt O-information—et mål for synergistiske og redundante interaktioner mellem hjernregioner—viste tidligt dynamiske ændringer med maksimale effekter i alpha-båndet efter 1 time (Cohen's d=2,57). Større stigning i 24-timers alpha-redundans var forbundet med symptomforbedring på dag 7 (β=69,31, q=0,05), hvilket antyder, at psykedelika kan være en neural markør for ketamin-respons.

items.title: Single Ayahuasca administration attenuates alcohol relapse and associated behavioral, neurochemical, and oxidative alterations in rats.

summaries.en: Preclinical study in rats examining single Ayahuasca administration's effects on alcohol relapse. After 35 days of voluntary ethanol consumption and 9-day deprivation, rats received Ayahuasca (2.72 mg/kg DMT plus β-carbolines) or placebo. Treatment reduced relapse-like drinking and improved anxiety/depression-like behaviors. Neurochemical analysis showed increased dopamine, serotonin, and BDNF in frontal cortex, hippocampus, and striatum; enhanced antioxidant enzyme activity; and reduced oxidative stress markers. Results suggest psychedelics may address multiple neurobiological mechanisms underlying alcohol use disorder.
summaries.da: Preklinisk dyrestudium af enkeltstående Ayahuasca-administration på alkoholrecidiv. Efter 35 dages frivilligt ethanolforbrug og 9 dages deprivation modtog rotter Ayahuasca (2,72 mg/kg DMT plus β-carboliner) eller placebo. Behandlingen reducerede relapsslignende alkoholindtag og forbedrede angst- og depressionsagtig adfærd. Neurochemisk analyse viste øget dopamin, serotonin og BDNF i præfrontal cortex, hippocampus og striatum samt forbedret antioxidant-enzymaktivitet og reducerede oxidativt stress-markører. Resultater tyder på, at psykedelika kan påvirke multiple neurobiologiske mekanismer bag alkoholforfbrugsforstyrrelser.

items.title: Toward a nuanced framework for the Medical development of ibogaine and its analogues and derivatives: implications for psychopharmacology.

summaries.en: Ibogaine, a naturally occurring alkaloid, shows therapeutic promise for substance use disorders, trauma, mood disorders, and suicidality, but clinical development faces safety and regulatory challenges. Researchers propose two complementary strategies: developing ibogaine analogs that preserve broad polypharmacological effects while reducing safety risks, and creating selective 'bespoke' compounds targeting specific conditions like opioid use disorder or PTSD. The review evaluates evidence for each approach and discusses trade-offs regarding safety, efficacy, and scalability, emphasizing the critical role of pharmacogenetics and individual variability in treatment outcomes.
summaries.da: Ibogain, et naturligt alkaloid, viser terapeutisk potentiale for stoffmisbrug, trauma, humørstoornelser og selvmordsfaren, men klinisk udvikling møder sikkerhed og regulatoriske udfordringer. Forskere foreslår to komplementære strategier: udvikling af ibogain-analoga, der bevarer bredt virkende effekter samtidig med at reducere sikkerhedsrisici, og skabelse af selektive 'skræddersyede' forbindelser målrettet specifikke tilstande som opioid-afhængighed eller PTSD. Reviewet evaluerer evidens for hver tilgang og diskuterer kompromiser vedrørende sikkerhed, effektivitet og skalerbarhed, med vægt på farmakogenetik og individuel variation.

items.title: Mitochondrial-inflammation crosstalk in major depressive disorder: molecular mechanisms and therapeutic implications.

summaries.en: This review examines mitochondrial dysfunction and neuroinflammation as central mechanisms in major depressive disorder (MDD). Mitochondrial abnormalities—including impaired energy production, compromised quality control, and redox imbalance—activate inflammatory pathways, creating a self-reinforcing cycle. Conventional antidepressants gradually restore mitochondrial function, while rapid-acting agents like ketamine trigger acute metabolic reprogramming. Emerging evidence supports mitochondria-targeted interventions, including antioxidants, metabolic modulators, and psychedelics, as therapeutically promising approaches.
summaries.da: Dette review undersøger mitokondriefunktionsforstyrrelser og neuroinflamation som centrale mekanismer i depression. Mitokondriefejl—herunder nedsat energiproduktion, påvirket kvalitetskontrol og redox-ubalance—aktiverer inflammatoriske veje og skaber en selvforstærkende cykel. Konventionelle antidepressiva genopretter gradvist mitokondriefunktion, mens hurtigvirkende midler som ketamin udløser akut metabolsk omprogrammering. Stigende evidens understøtter mitokondrie-målrettede interventioner, inklusiv antioxidanter, metabolske modulatorer og psykedelika, som terapeutisk lovende tilgange.

items.title: Rapid clinical improvement after intravenous ketamine in an adolescent with treatment-resistant depression, severe obesity, and pseudo-Cushing syndrome: A case report.

summaries.en: Case report documenting rapid clinical improvement in an adolescent with treatment-resistant depression, severe obesity, and pseudo-Cushing syndrome following intravenous ketamine administration. The case illustrates ketamine's potential therapeutic effects in complex comorbid presentations resistant to conventional antidepressant treatment.
summaries.da: Casusrapport, der dokumenterer hurtig klinisk forbedring hos en adolescent med behandlingsresistent depression, svær overvægt og pseudo-Cushings syndrom efter intravenøs ketamin. Casusen illustrerer ketamins potentielle terapeutiske effekt ved komplekse komorbiditeter, der er resistente over for konventionel antidepressiv behandling.

items.title: Psilocybin as a Transdiagnostic Treatment for Eating Disorders and Comorbid Psychopathology: Implications for Clinical Nosology and Research Directions.

summaries.en: This narrative review examines psilocybin treatment as a potential transdiagnostic intervention for eating disorders and comorbid psychiatric conditions. Preliminary clinical evidence supports feasibility, safety, and therapeutic effects, with transdiagnostic benefits observed across comorbidities. Proposed mechanisms include serotonergic agonism and psychoplastogenic effects targeting shared vulnerabilities aligned with HiTOP and RDoC nosological frameworks.
summaries.da: Denne narrative review undersøger psykedelika baseret på psilocybin som potentiel transdiagnostisk intervention for spiseforstyrrelser og komorbide psykiatriske tilstande. Foreløbige kliniske beviser støtter gennemførbarhed, sikkerhed og terapeutiske effekter med transdiagnostiske fordele på tværs af komorbiditeter. Foreslåede mekanismer inkluderer serotonergi og psykoplastogeniske effekter rettet mod delte sårbarhedsfaktorer i overensstemmelse med HiTOP og RDoC nosologiske rammer.

items.title: Semantic confusion between "MD" and MDMA in Indian Addiction Psychiatry Practice.

summaries.en: This article addresses terminological confusion in Indian addiction psychiatry between the abbreviations 'MD' and 'MDMA'. The distinction is clinically relevant for accurate diagnosis and treatment planning in psychiatric practice, particularly given varying terminology use across regions.
summaries.da: Artiklen behandler terminologisk forvirring inden for indisk afhængighedspsykiatri mellem forkortelserne 'MD' og 'MDMA'. Denne skelnen er klinisk relevant for korrekt diagnose og behandlingsplanlægning, især givet variationen i terminologibrug på tværs af regioner.

items.title: Consensus by design: Conflict of interest and the intravenous ketamine Delphi guidelines.

summaries.en: This article examines potential conflicts of interest in the development of intravenous ketamine Delphi guidelines. The study critiques the consensus-building process used to establish clinical recommendations for ketamine administration, questioning whether financial or professional interests may have influenced guideline development. The research highlights methodological concerns in how expert panels were selected and how consensus was achieved.
summaries.da: Artiklen undersøger potentielle interessekonflikter i udviklingen af Delphi-retningslinjer for intravenøst ketamin. Studiet kritiserer konsensusprocessen bag etablering af kliniske anbefalinger for ketaminadministration og stiller spørgsmål ved, hvorvidt økonomiske eller faglige interesser kan have påvirket guideline-udviklingen. Forskningen fremhæver metodologiske bekymringer ved udvælgelse af ekspertpaneler og konsensusdannelse.

items.title: Quarterly Psychedelics Briefing: Q2 2026

summaries.en: Psychedelic Alpha's Q2 2026 briefing surveys major developments across drug development, policy, regulation, and academic research. The quarterly update provides an overview of key industry trends and a forward-looking perspective on emerging priorities for the subsequent quarter.
summaries.da: Psychedelic Alphas Q2 2026-oversigt gennemgår vigtige udviklingen inden for lægemiddelsforskning, politik, regulering og akademisk forskning. Den kvartalsvise opdatering giver overblik over centrale branchens tendenser og perspektiver på kommende prioriteter.

items.title: Psychedelics and autism spectrum disorder: Mechanistic insights, translational evidence and ethical challenges.

summaries.en: A narrative review examining psychedelics as potential therapeutic agents for autism spectrum disorder. While psychedelics modulate serotonergic and glutamatergic signaling and enhance neuroplasticity, current evidence does not support treatment of core ASD symptoms. However, emerging evidence suggests potential benefits for associated comorbidities like anxiety and emotional dysregulation, particularly with psilocybin and ketamine. The review discusses mechanistic rationale and ethical challenges in translating these findings to clinical practice.
summaries.da: Et narrativt review, der undersøger psykedelika som potentielle terapeutiske agenser ved autismespektrumforstyrrelser. Selvom psykedelika modulerer serotonergt og glutamatergt signalering og øger neuroplasticitet, støtter nuværende evidens ikke behandling af kernegymnastiske symptomer. Dog viser stigende evidens potentielle fordele for ledsagende komorbiditet som angst og følelsesmæssig dysregulation, især med psilocybin og ketamin. Reviewet diskuterer mekanistisk rationale og etiske udfordringer ved klinisk translation.

items.title: Modeled Long-Term Effects of Psilocybin on Dynamic Activity and Effective Connectivity of Fronto-Striatal-Thalamic Circuits.

summaries.en: A computational modeling study of resting-state fMRI data from psilocybin-naive volunteers examined long-term brain mechanisms following a single high dose. Results showed increased fronto-striatal-thalamic circuit activity 4 weeks post-administration, driven by reduced structure-function coupling and shifts in information flow patterns. Modeling linked these changes to serotonergic and dopaminergic receptor effects, suggesting psilocybin enhances circuit flexibility through decreased cortical top-down control.
summaries.da: En computationel modelleringsstudie af hviletilstands-fMRI-data fra psykedelika-naive frivillige undersøgte langtidsmekanismer i hjernen efter en enkelt høj dosis psilocybin. Resultater viste øget aktivitet i fronto-striatal-thalamus-kredsløb 4 uger efter administration, drevet af reduceret struktur-funktionskoblng og skift i informationsflowmønstre. Modellering forbandt disse ændringer til serotonerge og dopaminerge receptoreffekter og tyder på, at psilocybin øger kredsløbsfleksibilitet gennem reduceret cortical top-down-kontrol.

items.title: Investigating the impact of serotonergic psychedelic drugs, MDMA and ketamine on social cognition in psychiatric disorders: A scoping review.

summaries.en: A scoping review of 20 studies examined how ketamine, MDMA, psilocybin, LSD, and ayahuasca affect social cognition in psychiatric disorders. Findings suggest ketamine and psilocybin may modulate facial emotion processing in depression, while MDMA showed improvements in self-awareness and self-compassion in PTSD. Evidence indicates psychedelics could modulate social cognition processes, though direct evidence of clinical benefit remains limited.
summaries.da: Et scoping review af 20 studier undersøgte, hvordan ketamin, MDMA, psilocybin, LSD og ayahuasca påvirker social kognition ved psykiatriske lidelser. Resultaterne tyder på, at ketamin og psilocybin kan modulere behandling af ansigtsudtryk ved depression, mens MDMA viste forbedringer i selvbevidsthed og selvmedlidenhed ved PTSD. Evidensen indikerer, at psykedelika kunne modulere sociale kognitiv processer, selvom direkte bevis for klinisk benefit er begrænset.

items.title: Study Protocol for "Exploring the safety and therapeutic potential of psilocybin in the treatment of anorexia nervosa in adolescents and young adults".

summaries.en: The psiAN study is a phase IIa randomized controlled trial investigating psilocybin-assisted therapy for treatment-resistant anorexia nervosa in adolescents and young adults (16-35 years). Participants receive either two 25 mg psilocybin doses with psychological support plus standard treatment, or standard treatment alone. Primary outcomes assess safety and tolerability; secondary outcomes measure eating disorder symptom severity, relapse rates, mood, and well-being through 12-month follow-up. Neurobiological mechanisms are explored via fMRI and biomarkers.
summaries.da: Undersøgelsen psiAN er et fase IIa randomiseret kontrolleret forsøg, der udforsker psykedelika-assisteret terapi for behandlingsresistent anoreksia nervosa hos unge (16-35 år). Deltagere modtager enten to doser á 25 mg psykedelika med psykologisk støtte plus standardbehandling eller kun standardbehandling. Primære resultater vurderer sikkerhed og tolerabilitet; sekundære resultater måler spiseforstyrrelses-symptomer, tilbagefald og velbefindende gennem 12 måneders opfølgning. Neurologiske mekanismer undersøges via hjernescanning og biomarkører.

items.title: Esketamine multi-omic biomarker evaluation in major depressive disorder (EMBER-MDD): concept, objectives and methodologies of a non-clinical investigator-initiated study.

summaries.en: EMBER-MDD is a non-interventional, in-vitro study analyzing biospecimens from ~420 adults with major depressive disorder to identify multi-omic biomarkers associated with treatment resistance and response to esketamine nasal spray versus treatment as usual. Using genomics, epigenomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics/metabolomics on blood samples collected at baseline and during/after treatment, the study aims to discover molecular signatures predicting treatment outcomes.
summaries.da: EMBER-MDD er en ikke-interventionel in-vitro-undersøgelse, der analyserer biologiske prøver fra cirka 420 voksne med alvorlig depression for at identificere multi-omiske biomarkører forbundet med behandlingsresistens og respons på esketamin-næsespray versus standard behandling. Ved hjælp af genomik, epigenomik, transkriptomik og proteomik/metabolomik på blodprøver indsamlet før og under/efter behandling sigter studiet på at opdage molekylære signaturer, der forudsiger behandlingsresultater.
